title: Address book index renders writable label flows before labels have actually loaded

evidence.quote: const { customEntries, getEntry, revalidate, isLoading, error, markPendingMutation, isMutationPending, markPendingReportMutation, isReportMutationPending, } = useAddressLabels();

during session-loading the provider doesn't fire SWR yet, so `isLoading` is false but `data` is still undefined → `hasLoaded` false. Without the gate, the form would render as blank-new and a fast save would PUT empty fields over an existing entry.

const mockUseAddressLabels = vi.fn(() => ({ customEntries: mockCustomEntries, getEntry: mockGetEntry, revalidate: mockRevalidate, isLoading: false as boolean, error: undefined as Error | undefined, ... }))

reasoning: The index page only gates on `isLoading` and never reads the provider's `hasLoaded` flag. The included detail-page tests document the provider contract: during session hydration or a first-read failure, `isLoading` can be false while labels have not resolved, and `getEntry()` returning undefined is ambiguous. In that state the index can show static contract rows and open `AddressLabelEditor` with `findContractInitial` or empty initial values, allowing a user to save over an existing custom label before the private labels read has completed. The AddressBookClient tests mock no `hasLoaded` field, so they cannot catch this first-load ambiguity.
reproduction: Navigate to `/address-book` while the session is still hydrating or force the first `/api/address-labels` read to fail before any successful load. Click `+ Tag` on a contract row or `+ Add label` for an address that already has a custom label, then save before labels resolve; the form is seeded as if no custom entry exists.
recommendation: Destructure `hasLoaded` from `useAddressLabels()` in `AddressBookClient` and block writable label modals/actions until it is true. Mirror the detail page behavior: show a loading placeholder while `!hasLoaded && !error`, show a blocking first-load error when `error && !hasLoaded`, and only preserve editable UI for background refresh errors after a successful load.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: `AddressBookClient.test.tsx` only tests `isLoading: true` and `error` branches; its default provider mock omits `hasLoaded`, so the session-hydration state described by the detail-page tests is never exercised on the index page.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an AddressBookClient test where `useAddressLabels()` returns `isLoading: false`, `hasLoaded: false`, `getEntry: undefined`, and seeded contract rows. Assert the page does not render writable `+ Tag`/edit flows or an `AddressLabelEditor`, and add a first-load-error case that blocks the form instead of showing a blank-new editor.

history.note: Fixed AddressBookClient to gate writable flows on hasLoaded and added a pre-load no-writable-controls regression.
